ogTitle: Molloy Island vs Monjingup: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Molloy Island and Monjingup.
heading: Molloy Island vs Monjingup — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Molloy Island, Western Australia and Monjingup, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Molloy Island has a lower overall climate risk score (31/100) compared to Monjingup (39/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Molloy Island or Monjingup safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Molloy Island has a flood risk score of 29/100, while Monjingup scores 58/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Molloy Island scores 35/100 for bushfire risk. Monjingup scores 36/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Molloy Island or Monjingup?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Molloy Island has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.4% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
